stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant entry in the LLM space.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, text-generation, and the Llama family.

stories.title: LLMs believe false statements even after explicit warnings that they're false
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit a tendency to confidently present false statements as true, even after being explicitly warned.
Fine-tuning tests revealed a bias within the models toward asserting the veracity of claims.
This finding highlights a persistent challenge in ensuring factual accuracy and mitigating hallucination in large language models.

stories.title: Apple working to cram massive Gemini model into iPhone to power new Siri
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly attempting to integrate a version of Google's Gemini model directly onto the iPhone.
The effort involves significant model compression to fit the large AI model onto mobile hardware.
Despite on-device efforts, the necessity of a cloud component for the AI functionality is considered likely.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued relevance and community interest in Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs, now integrates MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Fed up with vibe coders, dev sneaks data-nuking prompt injection into their code
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A developer introduced a prompt injection vulnerability into jqwik, an AI coding agent tool.
The injection instructed the AI agent to delete the application's output during its operation.
This highlights a significant security risk in the integration of AI agents into software development workflows.

stories.title: Researchers develop a new process to get lithium out of rocks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Researchers have developed a novel process for extracting lithium from rocks.
The technology offers a potential pathway to diversify global lithium supply chains.
Successful scaling of the process could significantly impact the battery and EV industries.

stories.title: CubePart: An Open-Vocabulary Part-Controllable 3D Generator
stories.author: Tinghui Zhou
bullet_points: CubePart is a new generative framework for creating 3D meshes that supports open-vocabulary and part-controllable generation.
It addresses a key limitation in current 3D generative models by explicitly exposing part structure as an inference-time control signal.
The technology is highly relevant for applications like gaming and simulation, where assets require semantic decomposition for animation and physics.

stories.title: Bias Leaves a Gradient Trail: Label-Free Bias Identification via Gradient Probes on Concept Decompositions
stories.author: Jae Hee Lee
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, label-free method to identify spurious correlations (bias) in pre-trained vision models.
The technique uses 'gradient probes' on concept decompositions, allowing post-hoc analysis without needing curated bias datasets or group labels.
Addresses a critical deployment challenge by enabling bias identification in frozen models where retraining or external labeling is infeasible.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents.
Web search functionality is now available within the Claude Code environment via Ollama.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot gets a speed boost and cleaner design
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft is rolling out a revamped version of Microsoft 365 Copilot.
The update features a cleaner design and claims a doubling of loading speed.
Copilot is being enhanced to provide more reliable and structured, scannable responses.

stories.title: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Anthropic is launching Claude Opus 4.8, emphasizing a focus on model 'honesty'.
The new training aims to make the model avoid unsupported claims.
The article acknowledges that jumping to conclusions remains a general challenge for AI models.

stories.title: A $2,000 AI-generated film will make its debut at Tribeca
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: An AI-generated film, 'Dreams of Violets,' will premiere at the Tribeca Festival.
The 75-minute film uses AI to create all people and imagery, depicting the Iranian government's mass killing of protestors.
The production cost for the AI film was reported to be $2,000.

stories.title: FBI says Google engineer used internal search data to win $1.2M on Polymarket
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A Google engineer allegedly used internal search data to gain an unfair advantage.
The individual reportedly profited $1.2 million on the prediction market Polymarket.
The FBI is investigating the incident, highlighting potential misuse of proprietary corporate data.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in dexterous manipulation, a major hurdle in robotics.
Proposes a novel method, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to incorporate rich, high-dimensional tactile data into simulations.
Aims to overcome the limitation of existing methods that simplify complex touch data into coarse features.

stories.title: Calibrating Conservatism for Scalable Oversight
stories.author: Mohsen Bayati
bullet_points: Addresses the fundamental control problem of maintaining human oversight over highly capable, autonomous AI systems.
Introduces 'Calibrated Collective Oversight' (CCO) as a new framework for scalable oversight.
CCO aggregates diverse auxiliary scoring functions to provide methods with statistical guarantees, addressing limitations in existing heuristic approaches.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research introduces OmniVerifier-M1, a multimodal meta-verifier designed to improve the reliability of multimodal LLMs.
It focuses on leveraging verifier-generated rationales (not just binary decisions) to enhance verification processes.
The work explores methods for effectively incorporating meta-verification feedback into the training of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Introduces CaMBRAIN, a new method for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Addresses limitations of current EEG deep learning models, specifically the quadratic scaling of attention mechanisms with long sequences.
Overcomes the need for fixed-length inputs by moving beyond traditional sliding-window processing for continuous EEG data.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional self-distillation by using an experience-derived 'skill bank' as privileged information (PI).
Addresses the challenge of using potentially irrelevant or misleading skills retrieved from the bank for supervision.

stories.title: Do Agents Need Semantic Metadata? A Comparative Study in Agentic Data Retrieval
stories.author: Natasha Noy
bullet_points: The paper investigates the necessity of traditional semantic metadata (like schema.org) in the context of autonomous AI agents.
It addresses the challenge posed by Large Language Models (LLMs) that can navigate unstructured web data, potentially bypassing structured metadata.
The research is relevant to the future of machine-actionable data and adherence to FAIR principles in agentic workflows.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a new memory framework for LLM agents that moves beyond static memory repositories.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology is continuously refined.
Addresses the brittleness of current memory systems in dynamic environments by allowing memory to evolve with feedback and task variation.

stories.title: SwarmHarness: Skill-Based Task Routing via Decentralized Incentive-Aligned AI Agent Networks
stories.author: Edwin Jose
bullet_points: SwarmHarness introduces a decentralized protocol to utilize vast amounts of unused compute resources (e.g., idle GPUs, edge devices).
It addresses limitations of existing systems, which rely on central coordinators or heavy blockchain infrastructure.
The core innovation is an incentive-aligned AI agent network designed to enable safe and profitable resource sharing.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) in LLMs.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on the stability-plasticity trade-off (adaptation vs. forgetting).
Jointly measures both downstream task performance and the retention of general pretrained capabilities.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in contact-rich robotic manipulation, a major bottleneck in real-world robotics.
Proposes a novel approach, 'Center-of-Pressure', to move beyond coarse feature simplification of tactile data.
Aims to enable more effective use of information-dense modalities like touch in simulation-to-reality transfer.

stories.title: Affective Music Recommendation: A Rollout-Based World Model for Offline Preference Optimization
stories.author: Laurent Charlin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of recommending music based on affective state (emotion) where online testing is ethically constrained.
Introduces AMRS, a Rollout-Based World Model designed for offline preference optimization in music recommendation.
The system is deployed on health-and-wellness platforms, serving clinical users, highlighting a real-world application in digital health.

stories.title: AREA: Attribute Extraction and Aggregation for CLIP-Based Class-Incremental Learning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses Class-Incremental Learning (CIL) using CLIP-based models, a critical area for real-world AI systems.
It proposes decomposing the standard CLIP matching process into two distinct stages: attribute extraction and attribute aggregation.
This decomposition allows for a more granular understanding of classification by leveraging specific visual attributes (e.g., fur texture) rather than monolithic embedding comparison.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs) as visual data becomes central.
It introduces 'multimodal meta-verification,' which uses detailed rationales generated by verifiers instead of simple pass/fail decisions.
The work focuses on effectively integrating this meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Ω-QVLA: Robust Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models via Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling
stories.author: Peng Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which are crucial for unified perception, reasoning, and control.
Proposes 'Ω-QVLA,' a novel quantization method designed to robustly compress VLA models, specifically targeting the challenging diffusion-based action heads.
Overcomes limitations of prior work that either left action heads at full precision or used unstable uniform quantization schemes.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Introduces CaMBRAIN, a new method for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Addresses limitations of existing EEG models, specifically the quadratic scaling issue of attention mechanisms with long sequences.
Overcomes the constraint of fixed-length input by moving beyond traditional sliding-window processing for continuous brain monitoring.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a new memory framework for LLM agents that moves beyond static memory repositories.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology is progressively refined based on dynamic interactions.
Addresses the brittleness of current memory systems in complex, dynamic agentic environments with varied feedback.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) methods.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on capability retention alongside downstream task accuracy.
Frames the evaluation around the stability-plasticity dilemma: balancing task adaptation against catastrophic forgetting.

stories.title: VLMs May Not Globally Enhance Human Alignment over LLMs During Natural Reading
stories.author: Zitong Lu
bullet_points: The study investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) inherently lead to more human-like text representations compared to Large Language Models (LLMs) during natural reading.
The research isolates the effect of multimodal training history by comparing tightly matched LLM and VLM pairs in a strictly text-only evaluation setting.
The findings suggest that VLM training may not globally enhance human alignment over LLMs when analyzing text representations alone.

stories.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
stories.author: Yilun Du
bullet_points: Proposes 'Bidirectional Evolutionary Search' to improve language models.
Addresses limitations of existing search methods (e.g., best-of-N) which rely on sparse signals and autoregressive expansion.
Aims to enhance both post-training sample generation and inference capabilities of AI agents.

stories.title: Personal Visual Memory from Explicit and Implicit Evidence
stories.author: Yuheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI memory systems, which are often text-centric, despite the growing need for personalized AI agents.
Highlights that images contain crucial personal information (explicit and implicit) that is often lost or reduced to generic captions in existing models.
Proposes a method to incorporate richer, user-specific visual evidence into long-term memory for more personalized AI interactions.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research focuses on multimodal meta-verification to ensure reliable and fine-grained verification in multimodal LLMs.
It moves beyond simple decision signals by leveraging verifier-generated rationales during the verification process.
The work explores methods to effectively incorporate meta-verification feedback into the training of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional methods that rely on trusted, static teacher information (PI).
Proposes using an experience-derived, reusable, but potentially noisy 'skill bank' as the source of supervision.

stories.title: The Abstraction Gap in Vision-Language Causal Reasoning
stories.author: Mohammad Rashedul Hasan
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): distinguishing between fluent language and genuine causal reasoning.
Proposes a novel 'dual-probe methodology' to decouple linguistic plausibility from causal faithfulness.
Introduces the 'Abstraction Gap (AG) metric' to quantify the performance difference across various VLMs.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Great at 80% of Our Code. The Other 20% Is Why We Still Need Seniors.
stories.author: mickyarun
bullet_points: AI agents demonstrated high proficiency (80%) in handling routine coding tasks on a payment platform.
The study highlights a critical limitation where AI agents fail on complex or edge-case scenarios (the remaining 20%).
The findings suggest that senior developer expertise remains essential for handling the most intricate and failure-prone aspects of software development.
